latest.title: DynamoDB Event Readiness, Part 4: SDK Configuration, Load Testing & Day-of Execution
latest.description: The last 10% is where events still fail. This final part covers SDK configuration, load testing strategy, and a day-of execution runbook — plus the full series checklist you can print and run before every major event. Part 4 of a 4-part DynamoDB event readiness series.

latest.title: DynamoDB Event Readiness, Part 3: Warm Throughput & Backup/DR Posture
latest.description: Warm throughput and backup/DR are the two areas teams most consistently skip — and both have day-of consequences. This part covers how to pre-provision partition capacity and build a rollback plan you can execute under pressure. Part 3 of a 4-part DynamoDB event readiness series.

latest.title: The Hidden Cost of AWS Lambda SnapStart for Python, and How I Fixed It with Durable Functions
latest.description: This article explores a hidden AWS Lambda cost issue caused by old SnapStart-enabled Python versions that were no longer being used but were still incurring cache charges. I walk through how I built a cleanup workflow using AWS Lambda Durable Functions to identify stale versions, generate a report, pause for human approval, and delete only after confirmation. It is a practical example of cost control with human-in-the-loop automation.

latest.title: DynamoDB Event Readiness, Part 2: Partition Key Analysis & Capacity Mode Validation
latest.description: Found a hot key? This part covers three fix patterns — write sharding, surrogate keys, and app-layer filtering — plus how to validate your capacity mode actually matches your event's traffic shape. Part 2 of a 4-part DynamoDB event readiness series.

latest.title: DynamoDB Event Readiness Part 1: Don't Let Your Biggest Day Become Your Worst Day
latest.description: "DynamoDB can handle any scale — but only if your tables are configured for it. This is Part 1 of a 4-part checklist series that walks you through preparing for major traffic events, starting with blast radius mapping and hot key detection

latest.title: Data Lake Serverless en AWS sin fallas silenciosas
latest.description: Evita pérdida de datos en data lakes serverless con observabilidad y DLQ en AWS.

latest.title: I Built a Local AI Agent. Here's What Cloud Taught Me Next.
latest.description: Local computing teaches you how things work. Cloud teaches you how to make them work for everyone else. Here's where I learned the difference.

latest.title: Minha Primeira Vez na Nuvem: Como o AWS Builder Center Começou a Definir Quem Eu Sou como Dev
latest.description: Como estudante de TI, meu primeiro contato com cloud computing foi uma mistura de encantamento e confusão. Neste artigo, compartilho como o AWS Builder Center me ajudou a encontrar minha identidade como desenvolvedor.

latest.title: Your Data Warehouse Just Became a Data Lake Writer — Apache Iceberg + Amazon Redshift
latest.description: Amazon Redshift can write directly to Apache Iceberg tables in S3. ACID-compliant. No ETL. No data copies. And the same data is immediately queryable by Athena, EMR, or Spark.

latest.title: Optimize WCU consumption of your AWS WAF WebACL
latest.description: AWS WAF costs scale directly with WebACL Capacity Units (WCUs), making rule optimization essential for high-traffic applications.

trending.title: AIdeas Finalist: Ivy - The World's First Offline-Capable, Proactive AI Tutoring Agent
trending.description: A proactive AI tutoring agent powered by Amazon Bedrock that monitors student progress, initiates outbound voice calls, and runs on-device inference for offline learning addressing educational inequity for millions of Ethiopian students with intermittent connectivity.

trending.title: AIdeas Finalist: Perspective
trending.description: Perspective is an AI-powered mental health companion that extracts clinical insight from free-form speech — no mood scales required. Using 4-cluster parallel extraction on Amazon Bedrock, validated against the Subjective Units of Distress Scale (SUDS), Work and Social Adjustment Scale (WSAS), PHQ-9, and GAD-7, it serves undiagnosed users and diagnosed patients alike. Clinician-validated by three practising doctors. Built on AWS serverless with Kiro.

trending.title: Construye con Strands Agents en Nerdearla Chile 2026
trending.description: Crea tu primer proyecto con Strands Agents en Nerdearla del 17 y 18 de Abril 2026 en Centro GAM, Santiago, Chile.

trending.title: Cómo crear un agente de IA desde cero. Open source, local y gratis.
trending.description: De un modelo que solo responde a uno que busca, razona y recuerda. Todo con código que puedes leer, forkear y contribuir.

trending.title: AIdeas Finalist: CareLens AI — Transparent Care Guidance When It Matters Most.
trending.description: CareLens is a privacy-first AI platform that helps people understand healthcare costs before seeking treatment. By analyzing de-identified symptoms, location, and optional insurance details, it compares care options like ER, urgent care, or specialists and surfaces real-world price ranges using hospital transparency and CMS data. It doesn’t diagnose—it delivers clear, grounded cost, bill analyzing and care guidance so users can make confident decisions without surprise bills.

trending.title: AIdeas Finalist: NeuroVoice
trending.description: Small moments, repeated daily, reveal what single snapshots never can - patterns that quietly signal neurological risk. NeuroVoice is a multimodal Parkinson’s screening platform that tracks pattern over time to detect subtle neurological changes early.

trending.title: AIdeas Finalist: RetainIQ
trending.description: Detect knowledge concentration risk before it costs you. RetainIQ scores every critical topic 0–100 and triggers AI interventions. Zero docs effort required.

trending.title: AIdeas Finalist: Aether Health
trending.description: Discover how our AI-powered platform reimagines the last mile of healthcare by focusing on empathetic symptom checking and prescription decoding. We provide verified, calming tools to combat health anxiety and prevent dangerous medication errors for vulnerable populations.

trending.title: AIdeas Finalist: Infinite Storefront – Eradicating the E-Commerce Return Crisis
trending.description: Infinite Storefront is a decentralized B2B2C sizing infrastructure eradicating the $890B e-commerce return crisis. Using a Multi-Axis Matrix and Amazon Bedrock, it proves garment fit mathematically before visual rendering. Built on 100% AWS serverless architecture, it equips independent boutiques with enterprise AI in under 4 minutes. By routing shoppers to local inventory for pickup, it bypasses reverse logistics entirely, turning retail margin protection into global climate action.

trending.title: AIdeas Finalist: Veloquity - The Agentic Evidence Intelligent Platform Turning Raw Feedback into Evidence-Driven Decisions
trending.description: Decisions don’t fail from too little data - they fail from too much noise. Veloquity, an agentic evidence intelligence platform, filters that noise by transforming fragmented feedback into validated evidence and delivering explainable, prioritized recommendations with full source-to-decision traceability that guides human decisions. READ ON TO SEE HOW.
